2606.20113.title: When Does Streaming Tool Use Help? Characterizing Tool-Intent Stabilization in Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation
2606.20113.paper_summary: Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation (Streaming RAG) reduces user-perceived latency by issuing tool queries in parallel with ongoing user input, before the utterance is complete. Reported gains are aggregate, yet the mechanism's benefit is fundamentally query-intrinsic: speculation can only help when the correct tool query becomes determinable before the user stops speaking or typing. We isolate and measure this property -- tool-intent stabilization, the point in the input stream at which a speculative query's retrieval converges to the answer-bearing result. On the CRAG benchmark (1371 validation questions) we (i) measure the distribution of stabilization, (ii) derive a model-agnostic bound H on the portion of tool latency that can be hidden behind the user's remaining input, as a function of tool latency L and input cadence δ, (iii) validate against a working streaming pipeline that realized savings meet or exceed this bound, and (iv) identify which query properties predict early versus late stabilization. The study requires no model training and runs on commodity CPU hardware. We find that at a realistic operating point (L=600ms, δ=3w/s, θ=0.8), 73.9% of queries across the full benchmark admit substantial latency hiding -- a blended figure that mixes sufficiency stabilization on the 21.3% of questions where gold evidence is verbatim-present and BM25-retrievable (95.2% streamable on this favorable slice) with a grounding-free top-1-settling fallback on the remainder. On the favorable slice, φ_suf is bracketed to [0.26, 0.281] by exact and relaxed grounding -- both early. Question type produces a significant but coarse early/late split (Kruskal-Wallis p=0.017, epsilon^2=0.04), directly informing when a learned speculative trigger is worth its cost.
2606.20113.paper_summary_zh: 串流檢索增強生成（Streaming RAG）通過在用戶輸入未完成之前並行發出工具查詢來減少用戶感知的延遲。報告的增益是總體的，但該機制的好處在根本上是查詢內在的：當正確的工具查詢在用戶停止說話或打字之前變得可確定時，推測才有幫助。我們隔離並測量這一特性——工具意圖穩定化，即在輸入流中，推測查詢的檢索收斂到承載答案的結果的點。在CRAG基準測試（1371個驗證問題）上，我們（i）測量穩定化的分佈，（ii）推導出一個與模型無關的界限H，該界限是可以隱藏在用戶剩餘輸入後的工具延遲部分，作為工具延遲L和輸入節奏δ的函數，（iii）驗證與一個運行中的串流管道，該管道實現的節省達到或超過這一界限，以及（iv）識別哪些查詢特性預測早期與晚期穩定化。該研究不需要模型訓練，並在商品CPU硬件上運行。我們發現，在一個現實的運行點（L=600ms，δ=3w/s，θ=0.8），全基準測試中73.9%的查詢允許顯著的延遲隱藏——這是一個混合數字，將在21.3%的問題上存在逐字呈現的金證據和可用BM25檢索的充分性穩定化（在這個有利的切片上95.2%可串流）與其餘部分的無基礎的前1個結果回退混合。在這個有利的切片上，φ_suf由精確和放鬆的基礎限制在[0.26, 0.281]之間——兩者都是早期的。問題類型產生了顯著但粗略的早期/晚期分裂（Kruskal-Wallis p=0.017，epsilon^2=0.04），直接告知何時學習的推測觸發器值得其成本。
2606.20113.author: Elroy Galbraith et.al.
